skill.name: go-rod-master
skill.description: Comprehensive guide for browser automation and web scraping with go-rod (Chrome DevTools Protocol) including stealth anti-bot-detection patterns.
skill.summary: Master browser automation and web scraping using go-rod, a native Chrome DevTools Protocol driver for Go with built-in stealth capabilities.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 279 potential issues but all are false positives from pattern matching on markdown documentation and Go import statements. The 'external_commands' findings are markdown table separators (|), not shell execution. The 'scripts' findings are Go import statements, not JavaScript. Network findings are example URLs in documentation, which is expected for a browser automation tutorial. One critical finding for 'keylogger keywords' at SKILL.md:515 references legitimate keyboard input simulation (page.Keyboard.MustType) for browser automation, not malicious keylogging. Screen capture findings reference screenshot functionality for testing. Overall risk is low due to legitimate browser automation use case with proper resource cleanup patterns documented.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external URLs
low_findings.description: Example code contains hardcoded URLs for demonstration purposes. This is expected behavior for a browser automation tutorial skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are example domains (github.com, example.com) used for demonstration in a browser automation tutorial - legitimate use case.

low_findings.title: Keyboard input simulation capability
low_findings.description: The skill documents keyboard input methods (MustType, MustPress) which could theoretically be misused but are standard browser automation APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyboard simulation is legitimate browser automation functionality for testing and form filling, documented with proper use cases.

low_findings.title: Screenshot and screen capture functionality
low_findings.description: Documentation includes screenshot methods (MustScreenshot, ScrollScreenshot) which capture page content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Screenshot functionality captures browser page content only, not system screen - standard testing/automation feature.

low_findings.title: Proxy configuration support
low_findings.description: Documentation shows SOCKS5 proxy configuration for routed traffic, which could enable traffic obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proxy support is legitimate for geo-distributed testing and scraping, shown with localhost example for development.

content.user_title: Automate browsers and scrape websites with go-rod
content.value_statement: Struggling with bot detection and complex browser automation in Go? This skill provides production-ready patterns for headless browser control with built-in stealth capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Navigate websites and extract content using CSS selectors, XPath, and text matching
Apply stealth evasions to bypass bot detection (remove navigator.webdriver, spoof WebGL, fix plugins)
Intercept and modify network requests with the HijackRequests API
Run concurrent browser sessions using page pools with proper resource management
Capture screenshots and PDFs of web pages with custom regions
Handle dynamic content with auto-wait, idle detection, and race selectors
limitations: Does not include CAPTCHA solving - requires external services for CAPTCHA challenges
Extensions require headed mode and do not work in headless Chrome
Each browser instance consumes 100-300MB RAM - concurrency should be limited on memory-constrained systems
Only supports Chromium-based browsers, not Firefox or Safari

use_cases.title: Web Scraping Pipeline
use_cases.description: Build reliable scrapers for dynamic JavaScript-heavy websites that load content via AJAX. Use stealth mode to avoid detection and page pools for concurrent scraping.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building ETL pipelines for web data collection

use_cases.title: Automated Testing
use_cases.description: Create end-to-end browser tests for web applications with proper wait strategies, element interaction, and screenshot capture for visual regression.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers testing React, Vue, or Angular single-page applications

use_cases.title: Bot Detection Research
use_cases.description: Study and test anti-bot detection systems by comparing fingerprint results before and after applying stealth evasions.
use_cases.target_user: Security researchers analyzing browser fingerprinting techniques

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Scraping
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Go script using go-rod that navigates to a news website, waits for the page to load, and extracts all article headlines with their links. Include proper error handling and resource cleanup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Extract structured data from a simple website

prompt_templates.title: Stealth Scraping with Anti-Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-ready scraper using go-rod/stealth that can bypass basic bot detection. Include proxy configuration, randomized delays between actions, and verification using a bot detection test page.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Scrape websites with basic anti-bot measures

prompt_templates.title: Network Request Interception
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a go-rod script that intercepts all API calls made by a single-page application, logs request/response data, and modifies response bodies to inject custom JavaScript tracking.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Monitor and modify network traffic for debugging or enhancement

prompt_templates.title: Concurrent Page Pool Scraper
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a high-performance scraper using rod.PagePool that processes a list of 100+ URLs concurrently with a maximum of 5 simultaneous pages. Include result aggregation, error handling, and proper cleanup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: Scale scraping operations with resource limits

output_examples.input: Navigate to GitHub and extract repository information
output_examples.output:
Page title: GitHub: Let's build from here
Found 127 links on the page
Search result: go-rod/rod - DevTools Protocol driver for Go

output_examples.input: Verify stealth mode passes bot detection tests
output_examples.output:
Screenshot saved to stealth_result.png
  User Agent: true
  WebDriver: missing (passed)
  Chrome: present (passed)
  Plugins Length: 3
  Languages: en-US,en

best_practices: Always use stealth.MustPage() instead of browser.MustPage() for real-world sites to avoid bot detection
Use defer browser.MustClose() immediately after connecting to ensure cleanup even on errors
Replace time.Sleep() calls with Rod's built-in wait methods like MustWaitStable() and MustWaitRequestIdle()
anti_patterns: Creating a new Browser instance per task - create one Browser and use multiple Page instances
Using Must* methods in production code - use error-returning methods for explicit error handling
Forgetting to call go router.Run() after setting up hijack routes - the router must be started

faq.question: How do I run go-rod without downloading Chromium?
faq.answer: Use launcher.NewBrowser().MustGet() to pre-download, or set the Launcher.Leakless(false) option. You can also connect to an existing Chrome instance using its WebSocket URL with rod.New().ControlURL(wsURL).MustConnect().

faq.question: Why am I still getting detected by Cloudflare after using stealth?
faq.answer: Stealth handles common fingerprints but advanced systems may require additional measures: use residential proxies, add human-like delays between actions, randomize viewport sizes, and implement behavioral patterns like random scrolling and hovering.

faq.question: How do I handle iframes and shadow DOM elements?
faq.answer: Use page.MustSearch() instead of page.MustElement() - it searches across all iframes and shadow DOM boundaries like DevTools Ctrl+F. Alternatively, switch context to the iframe first using page.MustElement("iframe").MustFrame().

faq.question: What is the difference between MustWaitLoad() and MustWaitRequestIdle()?
faq.answer: MustWaitLoad() waits for the page load event, while MustWaitRequestIdle() waits until there are no pending network requests. For AJAX-heavy pages, MustWaitRequestIdle() is more reliable as content may load after the initial page load.

faq.question: Can I use Chrome extensions with go-rod?
faq.answer: Yes, but extensions require headed mode. Use launcher.New().Set("load-extension", "/path/to/extension").Headless(false).MustLaunch() to load extensions. Headless mode does not support extensions.

faq.question: How do I handle file downloads?
faq.answer: Use browser.MustWaitDownload() which returns a wait function. Call it before triggering the download, then after clicking the download link, call the wait function to get the downloaded file data as a byte slice.
